title: Acme: Private Deployment — architecture deep-dive and calendar coordination
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive

Hi Priya —

Thanks again for the time on the call last week. The team asked for a focused architecture deep-dive to cover VPC/on-prem connectivity, air-gap constraints, and our update/patching model so your security and infra teams can validate operational fit.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue Jul 28, 10:00–11:30
- Wed Jul 29, 08:00–09:30
- Thu Jul 30, 13:00–14:30

Please let me know which works and who from your side should be on the call (security, network, procurement).

We'll have Soojin (solutions engineer) and Marissa (platform engineer) join. I attached a short checklist and our standard network diagram to give your team context. If there are any constraints (no internet egress, HSM-only KMS, private key custody), flag those and we’ll tailor the agenda.

Attachments: redwood_private_deploy_checklist.pdf, acme_network_diagram_v1.png

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123



From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 14:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive

Hi Camila —

Thanks for the proposal. We want to include our lead security engineer (Liam) and procurement (Alice) so both compliance and commercial folks can see the scope. Our team is particularly keen on: air-gapped update flow, KMS/HSM options, audit log retention, and egress controls.

From our side these slots work best (PT):
- Tue Jul 28, 10:00–11:30 (preferred)
- Thu Jul 30, 13:00–14:30 (backup)

If you can confirm attendees and share a lightweight agenda that would be great. Also — can you confirm whether 3rd-party monitoring requires outbound access, and whether you support reconnect-only update windows for air-gapped installs?

Thanks,
Priya Shah
Head of Security Operations | Acme Corp
priya.shah@acme-corp.com



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — internal prep

Team —

Forwarding Acme's reply. They prefer Tue Jul 28 at 10:00 PT. Can you confirm who is available to cover: VPC peering/topology, HSM/KMS options, air-gap update processes, and audit/log retention?

Key asks from Acme:
- Confirm whether we can operate with no outbound egress except for managed update windows
- Detail for key management: customer KMS vs Redwood-managed HSM options
- Network topology sketches for VPC-to-on-prem and firewall rules
- What logs/audit evidence we can provide for SOC2-style review (S3 retention windows, syslog exports)

If someone can draft a 45–60 minute agenda and attach any pre-read (network diagram + checklist), I'll send the calendar invite and include procurement.

Thanks —
Camila



From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — internal prep

Camila / all —

I'm available Jul 28 at 10:00 PT. I'll own the topology and KMS/HSM sections; Marissa can cover the air-gap update flow and patching model. Jordan can speak to networking (peering, routing), and Markus will be on the call for SRE-level SLAs and observability/export options.

Proposed agenda (60–75m):
1) Introductions & success criteria (5m)
2) High-level architecture (10m) — Jordan
3) Network & peering options (15m) — Jordan/Markus
4) KMS/HSM and key custody (10m) — Soojin
5) Air-gapped update/patching process & rollback (15m) — Marissa
6) Audit logs / evidence and retention (10m) — Markus
7) Q&A / next steps (10m)

I'll attach a short agenda doc and a checklist for items Acme should have available for the session. Also: we can support a reconnect-only model for updates (customer-triggered package staging + pull over temporary egress). We'll show the sequence and controls in the demo.

Attachments: private-deploy-agenda.docx

Soojin
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwood.ai



From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 23 Jul 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive

Hi Camila —

Quick procurement heads-up: we just moved an internal vendor review to Jul 28 and would prefer to delay the deep-dive by 24 hours so the right stakeholders can attend. Would Jul 29 at 10:00 PT (same length) work for your team? If not, Thu Jul 30 at 13:00 PT is still an option.

I'm attaching a short internal checklist for our security team in case it's useful. Also linking a shared calendar placeholder here: https://calendar.acme-corp.com/event/ACME-priv-deploy-placeholder

Thanks,
Priya



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jul 2026 13:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — confirmed new time

Thanks Priya — Jul 29, 10:00–11:15 PT works for our team. I've created a Google Meet and shared a Drive folder with pre-reads: network diagram, checklist, and the agenda Soojin drafted. Please find the links below.

Meeting: Jul 29, 10:00–11:15 PT
Google Meet: https://meet.google.com/xyz-acme-deepdive
Pre-reads: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-acme-private-deploy
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345/ACME-2026-42

Agenda summary: 60–75 minutes covering topology, KMS/HSM options, air-gapped update workflows, and audit evidence. We'll send the formal invite to calendar and include an attendee list. If there are network diagrams or firewall rules you want to share ahead of time, please drop them in the Drive folder.

See you all next week.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai

title: MAP kickoff: contracting milestones & terms owners
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Nora Patel <nora.patel@nimbushealth.com>, Amit Shah <amit.shah@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: contracting milestones & terms owners

Hi Nora / Amit —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a short mutual action plan (MAP) to align contracting milestones, owners, and a proposed close timeline so we can keep momentum toward an August close. Please find a lightweight MAP attached (Redwood_MAP_v1.xlsx) and a copy of our standard SaaS terms for reference.

Key asks from our side:
- Confirm your legal/procurement owners for redlines and PO execution (we've included Nora and Amit but list any additional reviewers)
- Confirm whether a DPA or SOC2 artifact is required prior to signature (we can surface SOC2 Type II report or SAS 70 equivalent if helpful)
- Agree target sign date: proposed target = 2026-08-31 (flexible +/- 7 business days if needed)

Proposed owners (please correct if inaccurate):
- Commercial: Marissa Cole (AE) — overall deal owner and procurement coordinator
- Legal / Terms: Rishi Malhotra (Redwood Legal) — primary for redlines and term negotiations
- Customer Success / Onboarding: Lauren Bishop — kickoff plan after signature

Quick contracting milestones we propose in the MAP:
1) 2026-07-22: MAP kickoff (today) — confirm owners and evidence list
2) 2026-07-27: Exchange initial redlines (NBD)
3) 2026-08-03: Legal alignment / finalize DPAs if required
4) 2026-08-17: Final internal approvals and signature queue
5) 2026-08-31: Target signature and kick-off onboarding

Attachments: Redwood_MAP_v1.xlsx (lightweight milestones + owners), Draft_SAAS_Terms.docx
Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cRedwoodMAP

If that timeline looks ok to you, please reply with your procurement/legal contacts and any mandatory procurement steps (PO template, vendor questionnaire link, or procurement portal).

Thanks — I’ll also loop Rishi to outline our redline process.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0181

--



From: Nora Patel <nora.patel@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Amit Shah <amit.shah@nimbushealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: contracting milestones & terms owners

Ben — thanks for the clear plan and the MAP. We’re excited to move forward.

Quick answers from our side:
- Legal/procurement owners: Nora Patel (me, legal) and Amit Shah (procurement lead) — I’ll be the primary redline owner.
- Evidence: We do want a SOC2 Type II report (latest) and a brief data flow diagram showing where PHI/PII is processed and stored. If you can share SOC2 and a short artifact that would speed our internal review.
- PO: We will need a PO for payment; procurement will provide our PO template when we’re closer to signature.

Proposed timeline looks reasonable. I’ll circulate to our security team and get back if they need more artifacts. Please have Rishi reach out on redline cadence.

Thanks,
Nora
Nora Patel
General Counsel | Nimbus Health
nora.patel@nimbushealth.com
+1 (212) 555-0199

On Thu, Jul 22, 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Nora / Amit —
> 
> Thanks again for the time yesterday. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a short mutual action plan (MAP) to align contracting milestones, owners, and a proposed close timeline so we can keep momentum toward an August close. Please find a lightweight MAP attached (Redwood_MAP_v1.xlsx) and a copy of our standard SaaS terms for reference.
> 
> Key asks from our side:
> - Confirm your legal/procurement owners for redlines and PO execution (we've included Nora and Amit but list any additional reviewers)
> - Confirm whether a DPA or SOC2 artifact is required prior to signature (we can surface SOC2 Type II report or SAS 70 equivalent if helpful)
> - Agree target sign date: proposed target = 2026-08-31 (flexible +/- 7 business days if needed)
> 
> Best,
> Ben


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Nora Patel <nora.patel@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: contracting milestones & terms owners

Hi Nora / team — looping in Redwood legal (that’s me). Thanks Ben for the MAP. A few notes on our redline process to keep expectations aligned:

1) Redline exchange cadence: we typically exchange a single consolidated redline pass (tracked changes) within 3 business days of receiving the counterpart’s annotated terms. If there are multiple concurrent reviewer threads, please route comments into one document to avoid drift.
2) Core negotiable items we expect to discuss: indemnity caps, data processing terms, securitying requirements (SOC2/DPA), and liability carve-outs for third-party models.
3) Timeline: if Nimbus returns redlines by 2026-07-27 we can respond with our counter by 2026-07-30 and aim for final alignment the week of 2026-08-10.
4) Preferred exchange method: we can accept annotated Word docs via Drive or tracked redlines via DocuSign. If Nimbus prefers DocuSign redlines, please provide a template link and we’ll push our standard template (Draft_SAAS_Terms.docx).

I’ve attached our Redline_Guidelines.pdf that explains formatting and preferred metadata in comments (author + section tags). Also including a link to the MAP in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cRedwoodMAP

If legal needs to speak directly, I’m available next Monday afternoon PST for a 30-min sync.

Regards,
Rishi Malhotra
Lead Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com

On Thu, Jul 22, 2026 at 11:03, Nora Patel <nora.patel@nimbushealth.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for the clear plan and the MAP. We’re excited to move forward.
> 
> Quick answers from our side:
> - Legal/procurement owners: Nora Patel (me, legal) and Amit Shah (procurement lead) — I’ll be the primary redline owner.
> - Evidence: We do want a SOC2 Type II report (latest) and a brief data flow diagram showing where PHI/PII is processed and stored. If you can share SOC2 and a short artifact that would speed our internal review.
> - PO: We will need a PO for payment; procurement will provide our PO template when we’re closer to signature.
> 
> Thanks,
> Nora


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Nora Patel <nora.patel@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Amit Shah <amit.shah@nimbushealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jul 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: contracting milestones & terms owners

Nora / Amit —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and clear asks. To confirm next steps: 
- I’ll upload the SOC2 Type II report and a short data-flow diagram to the Drive folder by EOD today.
- Marissa will collect your PO template and procurement contact details; please send when ready.
- Rishi, thanks for the guidelines — let’s target the 2026-07-27 redline exchange. If Nimbus prefers, we can accept a single annotated Word or DocuSign markup.
- Lauren will prepare an onboarding stub that we can use immediately after signature so we can shorten time-to-value.

If everyone is aligned, I’ll mark the MAP as "In Progress" and update milestone owners in the spreadsheet.

Best,
Ben
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0181

Hi Nora / team — looping in Redwood legal (that’s me). Thanks Ben for the MAP. A few notes on our redline process to keep expectations aligned:

1) Redline exchange cadence: we typically exchange a single consolidated redline pass (tracked changes) within 3 business days of receiving the counterpart’s annotated terms. If there are multiple concurrent reviewer threads, please route comments into one document to avoid drift.

4) Preferred exchange method: we can accept annotated Word docs via Drive or tracked redlines via DocuSign. If Nimbus prefers DocuSign redlines, please provide a template link and we’ll push our standard template (Draft_SAAS_Terms.docx).

I’ve attached our Redline_Guidelines.pdf that explains formatting and preferred metadata in comments (author + section tags). Also including a link to the MAP in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cRedwoodMAP

title: Nimbus: Routing for Third‑Party IP Claims & Escrow point-of-contact
preview:
From: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Ben — thanks for sending the draft order form and the MSA redline. Our counsel flagged two items that need quick alignment before we escalate to procurement: defend/control allocation for third‑party IP claims and the proposed escrow activation point for remediation costs.

Two quick questions so we can pull legal ops together:
- Who would be the operational contact at Redwood for evidence intake (triage + custody) if a claim is noticed?
- Can you confirm whether Redwood expects full defend-and-control for 3rd‑party infringement claims, or a defend-with-right-to-step-in model?

I've attached the MSA redline you sent and a short summary of our position. Paul (cc) will be the procurement lead on our side. If possible we’d like a brief call with legal ops tomorrow.

Thanks,
Sara Greene
Head of Legal & Ops
Nimbus Therapeutics

Attachments: Nimbus_MSA_redline_v3.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Sara — appreciate the note. High level posture from Redwood (so legal ops can prep):
- We propose defend-and-control for third‑party IP claims we are alleged to cause, with a reimbursement path for amounts we successfully defend against (typical defend-vs-reimburse mechanics).
- We are open to narrow carveouts where customer-provided training data or customer-integrated third‑party models change the allocation.
- Escrow activation: we propose escrow only for remediation costs that exceed a $250k threshold (we can discuss the threshold), not for routine defense fees.

Operational contacts:
- Evidence intake & custodian: Marcus Lin (marcus.lin@redwood.com) — he runs Contract Ops and will own evidence staging.
- Legal lead on Redwood side: Kimberly Park (kimberly.park@redwood.com).

Can Kimberly weigh in on specific redline language? Paul — would you share the internal budget/timing constraints so we can evaluate the escrow threshold tradeoff?

Next steps I propose:
1) Kimberly to propose clean redline language for Section 11 (IP claims) and an evidence checklist.
2) 30‑minute call tomorrow to confirm contact points and timeline.

-Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Nimbus_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Sara / Ben / Paul — following Ben's note, attaching a short, marked paragraph for the MSA Section X (IP Indemnity) that implements our defend-and-control approach while preserving customer obligations to cooperate. Key points:
- Redwood will have primary defense control for covered third‑party IP claims; we will not settle adversely to the customer's material rights without consent (consent not to be unreasonably withheld).
- Customer must provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation; failure to provide timely notice that prejudices Redwood's defense can limit recovery.
- Remedies limited to direct damages and remediation costs; consequential damages excluded per Section Y.

We prefer escrow language that limits activation to remediation/correction costs (coding fixes, redeployments, content takedown) that are demonstrably caused by Redwood's deliverable and above the specified threshold. We are flexible on the threshold but need a bright‑line.

I will upload a redline (short paragraph) to the DocuSign link Ben shared and can join the call to talk through exact phrasing. If you have proposed alternate language, paste it in-line and I will show the delta.

Regards,
Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redline_SectionX_sample.txt


From: Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-21T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Kimberly / Ben — thanks, that helps. A couple of counterpoints from Nimbus legal/procurement: 
- Nimbus is uncomfortable ceding full defend-and-control without an explicit right to take over if we reasonably conclude Redwood is not defending promptly or in our interest. We propose a 10‑business day cure window after written notice before Nimbus may assume defense.
- Regarding escrow: our preference is a lower activation threshold ($100k) or an escrow funding mechanics (pro rata deposits) — because remediation for some regulated products is front-loaded.

On evidence: can Marcus confirm whether Redwood will accept scanned chain-of-custody plus app logs and whether you require source assets? We can prepare the sample packet once we know the list.

If useful, happy to do a 30‑minute legal ops call tomorrow morning.

—Paul
Director, Sourcing & Contracts
Nimbus Therapeutics

- Nimbus is uncomfortable ceding full defend-and-control without an explicit right to take over if we reasonably conclude Redwood is not defending promptly or in our interest. We propose a 10‑business day cure window after written notice before Nimbus may assume defense.
- Regarding escrow: our preference is a lower activation threshold ($100k) or an escrow funding mechanics (pro rata deposits) — because remediation for some regulated products is front-loaded.

On evidence: can Marcus confirm whether Redwood will accept scanned chain-of-custody plus app logs and whether you require source assets? We can prepare the sample packet once we know the list.

If useful, happy to do a 30‑minute legal ops call tomorrow morning.

—Paul

Nimbus Therapeutics

title: Hosted API pricing: Catalyst Labs commit proposal & custom rate request
preview:
From: Sara Patel <sara@catalyst-labs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Miguel Torres <miguel@catalyst-labs.com>, procurement <procurement@catalyst-labs.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood hosted proposal — next steps

Ben,

Thanks for the detailed proposal and the usage projection — helpful to see both the steady-state and peak scenarios. Couple of asks from our finance/procurement side before we proceed to legal: 

- The blended per-token numbers you sent are above our target (we were budgeting closer to $0.0006/ token for steady-state). Can you swing closer to that on a committed program?
- We need clarity on how overage is billed versus committed tiers and whether there are any guaranteed latency/availability targets tied to the pricing.

We expect ~18 month consumption lifecycle across a phased rollout. If you can put together a firm commit + preferred-rate path (with start/end dates) we can move to SOW review. Attaching our internal projection for reference.

Attachment: Catalyst_usage_projection.xlsx (XLSX, 76KB)

—Sara
Director of Product, Catalyst Labs

> Hi Sara / Miguel,
>
> Great to speak yesterday. Attaching the standard hosted proposal and our 2026 rate card for reference. For the volumes you projected we recommended a 3-tier commit: 1) Proof-of-concept (30d, capped), 2) Ramp (3 months), 3) Committed steady-state (12 months) with blended per-token pricing listed on page 2.
>
> Attachment: redwood-hosted-ratecard-2026.pdf (PDF, 320KB)
> Attachment: Catalyst_SOW_v1.pdf (PDF, 210KB)
>
> Happy to rework numbers if you have target per-token figures.
>
> Thanks,
> Ben
> Account Executive — Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Redwood hosted proposal — next steps

Laura / Karthik — looping you in. Catalyst (contact: Sara Patel) pushed back on our steady-state per-token; they indicated internal target ~$0.0006/token, above what I proposed on the ratecard. They plan an 18-month consumption lifecycle and want a firm commit + preferred-rate path.

Their projection (attached) shows monthly variability: big weekly peaks tied to batch inference jobs. Current ask: can we author an exception path or blended commit that meets their unit target? They’re willing to sign 12-mo minimum if pricing is right.

Key ask from me: approval to offer a limited 12-month promotional concession (if we can protect margin with a committed minimum and review clause). If approved I’ll propose: 12-month commit, 3-tier usage breakpoints, 15% off the hosted per-token list for first 9 months, then revert to standard blended rates; include a cap on total monthly overage charges to avoid unlimited tail spend.

Numbers: Catalyst steady-state projected tokens/month ~1.8B; peak months up to ~3.5B. I’ve attached the spreadsheet they sent.

Attachment: Catalyst_usage_projection.xlsx (XLSX, 76KB)

Can you advise runway/limits for a concession and any required finance/legal approvals?

Thanks — Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Redwood hosted proposal — next steps

Ben — thanks for flagging. A few points before we commit to a concession: 

1) Margin floor — finance needs a modeled blended unit margin not lower than 22% over the 12 months. Can you provide a 12-month projection (monthly tokens, proposed concession, expected revenue) so we can validate?
2) Contract guardrails — any concession must be scoped to Hosted API only (no dedicated/private), include a 6-month business review clause, and an explicit overage definition (monthly measurement window + notification triggers).
3) Duration & ramp — if they’ll sign 12 months we can consider a deeper short-term concession for months 1–6 (to help with adoption) that steps up thereafter.

If Ben can get us the monthly projection, I’ll put a formal recommendation to pricing. Also request that legal include a clause preventing transfer of rates to other Catalyst subsidiaries.

Karthik
Head of Sales Strategy — Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@catalyst-labs.com>, Miguel Torres <miguel@catalyst-labs.com>, procurement <procurement@catalyst-labs.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 08:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood hosted proposal — next steps

Sara — quick update from our side after internal review. We can move toward a concession but need a small set of guardrails. Proposed term for discussion: 

- Contract length: 12 months; Hosted API only.
- Commit: Minimum 1.2B tokens/month for first 6 months; step to 1.8B tokens/month for months 7–12 (Catalyst can prepay monthly or keep monthly invoicing).
- Promotional pricing: 15% off listed hosted per-token rates for months 1–6, then 7% off months 7–12 (blended effectively across year).
- Overage: charged at list rate + 10% premium beyond the committed monthly quantity; Redwood to send automated usage alerts at 75% and 95% thresholds.
- SLA: standard hosted latency targets apply; we will provide an uptime SLA appendix in SOW.
- Review: 6-month health check with right to renegotiate if usage falls >25% below committed levels.

If that looks workable, I’ll get a formal SOW revision and a DocuSign route.

Attachment: Catalyst_SOW_v2.pdf (PDF, 244KB)

—Ben
Account Executive — Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood hosted proposal — next steps

Ben / Karthik — I reviewed the projection and the margin model (aditya ran the blended margin calc). Approve the concession with the following firm constraints: 

- Approve the 15%/7% stepped concession on the condition Catalyst commits to at least a 12-month contract and _either_ monthly prepay for the first 3 months _or_ a bank guarantee instrument.
- Cap the promotional exposure to the first 9 months (i.e., no additional custom discounts past month 9 without renewed approval).
- Require a clause that rates are non-transferable to affiliates and non-cumulative (savings apply only to the committed token bucket).
- Legal to include the 6-month business review and a termination-for-low-usage clause (if they deliver <50% of the committed tokens over a trailing 90-day window).

Finance will accept the blended margin with these guardrails. Ben — you have approval to present the revised SOW and DocuSign link to Catalyst and start legal redline. Copy Karthik and Aditya on the loop.

Laura Bennett
VP, Global Sales — Redwood Inference

- Review: 6-month health check with right to renegotiate if usage falls >25% below committed levels.

title: Deployment footprint: data handling & isolation checklist
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Wong <amelia.wong@novahealth.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@novahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Deployment footprint: data handling & isolation checklist

Hi Amelia / Raj —

Thanks again for the time this morning. Quick recap and a short checklist so we can land on whether Dedicated or Private VPC (or on-prem) is the right fit for NovaHealth:

Summary of your current posture (per call):
- Patient PII flows into your analytic pipeline; any vendor-hosted inference must not persist raw inputs beyond immediate processing.
- You'd prefer customer-controlled keys for any encrypted data at rest.
- Regulatory note: some workloads must be kept within US-only regions.

Key questions to confirm (short answers would be great):
1) BYOK: Do you require customer-managed KMS/HSM for model input/data at rest and for model artifacts? (yes/no)
2) KV/prefix caching: Are transient KV caches or prompt prefixes allowed to be stored outside your network for less than 24 hours, or must they never leave your VPC/on-prem?
3) Audit & Retention: Minimum required retention for audit logs and request traces (e.g., 90 days, 7 years)?
4) Egress & Approved Cloud: Any disallowed cloud providers or explicit allowlist?
5) Admin access: Do you require that vendor sysadmins have zero access to production data (i.e., full just-in-time access via bastion + auditable sessions) or limited privileged access is acceptable?
6) Throughput/latency SLOs: expected qps and 95th pct latency targets for critical endpoints (so we can size Dedicated pools).

I've attached a one-page checklist (novahealth-deployment-answers.xlsx) that mirrors the questions above — fill what you can and we can slot next steps.

Recommended next step from our side: if BYOK + strict KV controls + zero vendor access are required, Private VPC or on-prem will be the likely path. If you can accept customer-owned KMS + network egress controls to an approved region, Dedicated capacity in a customer VPC with strict IAM may satisfy both compliance and cost needs.

Would love a short reply with the six answers above by EOD Tue so we can scope an accurate proposal.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0127

Attachments: novahealth-deployment-answers.xlsx (xlsx), deployment-checklist.pdf (pdf)

From: Amelia Wong <amelia.wong@novahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@novahealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deployment footprint: data handling & isolation checklist

Ben —

Thanks for the clear checklist. Answers below inline and I've uploaded a quick matrix to our shared drive.

1) BYOK: Yes — we require customer-managed keys (AWS KMS with customer key policy) for any persisted artifacts.
2) KV/prefix caching: No persistence outside our VPC for any prompt/kv material that could contain PII. Transient in-memory is fine but must not be checkpointed to vendor storage.
3) Audit & Retention: Audit logs for access and request metadata: 2 years for normal ops, 7 years for clinical workflows.
4) Egress & Approved Cloud: Only AWS and GCP us-regions are approved; Azure is not approved for clinical datasets.
5) Admin access: Vendor sysadmins must not have standing access to production data. Just-in-time access with ticketed approval and video/audit logs is required for any elevated access.
6) Throughput/latency SLOs: Baseline 60 qps for inference (peak 250 qps burst), 95th pct latency <= 300ms for the critical inference route.

A couple of clarifications: when you say "Private VPC", do you support a deployment where the entire inference cluster is deployed into our AWS account (VPC, subnets, private endpoints) with no public internet egress? Also curious how KV cache eviction works in Dedicated vs Private: can we guarantee immediate eviction on request/incident?

Upload: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3c/view

- Amelia
Amelia Wong
Head of Data Privacy & Security | NovaHealth
amelia.wong@novahealth.com

To: Amelia Wong <amelia.wong@novahealth.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@novahealth.com>

Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 08:30:00 -0700

Amelia — thanks for the thorough answers, super helpful. Quick replies to your clarifications plus next steps: 

Private VPC model: yes — we offer a Private deployment mode where the inference runtime and control plane components (if you choose) run inside your AWS account and VPC. That deployment model supports private subnets, no public internet egress, and optional private endpoints for telemetry. We can also package an on-prem variant if your security team requires air-gapped infra.

KV cache eviction: for Dedicated we provide strong isolation and can set cache TTLs to near-zero, but Dedicated is still a Redwood-managed pool (we host in our infra). For Private (customer-account VPC) we can configure cache retention to be ephemeral in-memory only and support immediate eviction calls and incident wipe procedures. We will document the eviction API and provide a runbook for incident response.

Action items:
- I've attached a filled checklist to reflect your answers and added a few follow-ups (novahealth-deployment-answers.xlsx).
- Can you confirm who on your infra side (name + email) we should loop for a technical deep-dive?
- Marissa, can we schedule a 60-min technical sync with NovaHealth's infra + security next Wed or Thu?

If BYOK + strict KV/no-egress for PII are non-negotiable, we'll scope Private VPC as the default recommended path and include an on-prem option in the proposal. If they can accept our Dedicated model with customer-managed KMS and VPC peering, that gives a faster time-to-production with lower cost.

Thanks,
Ben

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Emma Rodriguez <emma@medtechsolutions.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)

Hi Emma,

Thanks again for a productive call this morning — appreciate you and Ron walking us through the production constraints. Quick recap and the asks we agreed to: 

- Current priorities: process PHI, steady-state ~10k requests/day, burst up to ~250 QPS.
- Key requirements you emphasized: tenant isolation (no cross-tenant KV leakage), audit logs for inference requests, and a data residency option.
- Target SLOs you mentioned: 99.9% availability and P50 generation latency <= 150ms for the primary inference path.

Open questions from our side that will shape the recommended deployment:
1) Do you consider processing PHI a hard requirement for Private deployment, or would Dedicated (with a customer-specific reserved pool + DPA) be acceptable if we can guarantee isolation?
2) Do you have an internal encryption/KMS requirement (customer-managed key) or would Redwood-managed keys be acceptable for the POC?
3) Any compliance paperwork we should start early (POC typically needs a redacted data mapping and either SOC2 or an attestation).

Proposed next steps:
- We can stand up a two-week Dedicated evaluation (isolated capacity with guaranteed GPU pools) using a sanitized dataset to validate noisy-neighbor behavior and latency distribution.
- If you need full PHI controls, we can scope a Private VPC deployment for a longer evaluation — that takes a bit more lead time.
- I'll loop in Laura (solutions) and Ben (dedicated ops) to draft the POC plan and provide SOC2 summary.

Are you available for a short 30-minute POC kickoff on Wed 21 Jul (US/Eastern) or Thu 22 Jul?

Thanks,
Rishi

--
Rishi Malhotra
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
rishi@redwood.com

Cc: Ron Smith <ron.smith@medtechsolutions.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 14:03:21 -0000
Subject: Re: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)

Thanks, Rishi — great summary. A few clarifications from our side: 

- PHI is a hard requirement for production. For the POC we can use de-identified data but we need to validate controls before we move to prod.
- We strongly prefer customer-managed KMS keys for production; for POC Redwood-managed is acceptable as long as we have a migration path.
- Our baseline SLO ask is 99.95% monthly availability and p50 <= 150ms; p95 target is <= 400ms. If Dedicated gives a consistent p95 we can be comfortable.

Noisy neighbor is our biggest operational fear — we want clear isolation boundaries and a traceable impact analysis if a neighbor causes tail latency. Can the POC include a simple \"traffic spike\" test where you drive synthetic load on a co-located tenant and we watch metrics?

We'll need a copy of your SOC2 summary and a short DPA template to share with legal. Ron and I are available Wed 21 Jul 10:00-11:30 ET for the kickoff.

Emma / Ron — great, thanks for the availability. Looping in Laura (solutions lead) and Ben (dedicated ops) to scope the two-week Dedicated evaluation and the traffic-spike test. See proposed plan below.

POC proposal (two-week Dedicated evaluation) — goals and high level: 
- Goal: Validate isolation, measure p50/p95 latency under representative workload, and exercise audit/logging and KMS flows.
- Environment: Dedicated reserved GPU pool (isolated nodes, non-shared tenancy) with a single customer namespace; Redwood will enable request-level audit logs and provide a metrics dashboard.
- Tests to run:
  1) Baseline latency profile with sanitized dataset (3 days of steady traffic).
  2) Noisy neighbor spike: we will introduce synthetic cross-tenant load on a co-located tenant to observe tail-latency impact.
  3) Failover: simulated node restart to validate graceful degradation and cache-warm behaviors.
  4) KMS flow: end-to-end check using Redwood-managed keys for POC; document steps for customer-managed KMS for production planning.
- Deliverables: test runbook, p50/p95 graphs, audit log samples, SOC2 summary, DPA template, recommended next-step path for Private VPC if required.

Logistics & timing:
- Kickoff: Wed 21 Jul 10:00 ET (you confirmed) — 30m.
- Prep: Redwood will share POC runbook and access instructions 24 hours prior.
- Start: Thu 22 Jul — run baseline tests and noisy-neighbor spike during second week.

Ben / Laura, can you confirm capacity: we need a small Dedicated pool (2 GPUs, NVIDIA A10/A30-class equivalent) reserved for two weeks starting Thu 22 Jul. Also attaching a draft POC plan and SOC2 summary for Emma to review.

Emma — attached are: redwood_poc_plan.pdf (draft runbook) and soc2-summary.pdf (SOC2 scope and attestation summary). I'll also share a short DPA template if legal wants it.

Thanks,
Rishi

Noisy neighbor is our biggest operational fear — we want clear isolation boundaries and a traceable impact analysis if a neighbor causes tail latency. Can the POC include a simple \"traffic spike\" test where you drive synthetic load on a co-located tenant and we watch metrics?

We'll need a copy of your SOC2 summary and a short DPA template to share with legal. Ron and I are available Wed 21 Jul 10:00-11:30 ET for the kickoff.

title: Commit credit swap and parity ask vs OpenAI/Anthropic
preview:
From: Alex Chen <alex.chen@bluepeak.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-20T14:05:00Z
Subject: Exploring a commit credit swap vs per-token parity with OpenAI

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the demo last week. Procurement asked me to get a concrete proposal on pricing options before we engage legal. Two asks up front: 1) can Redwood match or get close to OpenAI/Anthropic list/per-token economics for our hosted API usage (we run mostly text gen, about ~200M tokens/mo today, spiking to 500M in Q4), and 2) will you consider a "credit swap" structure — we prepay credits (shorter term) in exchange for a better effective per-token rate rather than a long-term reserved capacity contract?

For reference, we have competing offers: OpenAI is quoting roughly $0.0004/1k tokens for prompt+1k for completions at our projected volume (their tiered price), Anthropic is roughly in the same ballpark for instant endpoints once egress and context are factored. We're trying to avoid a heavy lift migration, but the unit economics matter.

Things we care about:
- Clear math (effective per-token after credits / rollovers)
- SLA + latency fallback (we need to avoid VM-induced regression)
- Dark-launch/test window and the ability to roll back if quality/latency regresses
- DocuSign + PO cadence (we prefer a 6-12 month contract minimum)

Attached our short RFP and expected run-rate. Can you send a couple of structured options (short credit-swap and a 12-month commit slab) and the sample commercial language your legal would need?

Appreciate it — looking to move quickly.

Alex
Director, Platform Engineering
BluePeak Analytics

Attachment: RFP-BluePeak-2026.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Chen <alex.chen@bluepeak.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-20T18:00:00Z
Subject: Re: Exploring a commit credit swap vs per-token parity with OpenAI

Alex —

Thanks for the clear ask and the RFP. We can do a concise set of options you can take to procurement/legal. High level (see attached pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx for the math):

Option A — 12-month committed slab
- 500M token committed over 12 months (monthly true-up), effective per-token: $0.00030 (applies to text generation workloads)
- SLA: 99.9% regional availability, priority routing, can include on-call escalation
- Rollover: up to 10% unused credits can roll quarter-to-quarter for the first year

Option B — 6-month credit-swap (prepaid usage credits)
- Prepay $X for 6 months worth of credits; effective per-token: $0.00035 (10% discount vs our standard hosted list)
- Credits consumed first; no overage discount beyond credits; unused credits roll for 60 days after term end
- Includes a performance review at 90 days with an SLA remediation clause (we can add a rollback / migration assistance clause if latency or throughput fall below agreed thresholds)

Why we can't promise exact 1:1 parity with OpenAI/Anthropic across the board
- Token accounting and model mixes matter — Anthropic/OpenAI endpoint pricing includes different model shapes and service semantics; effective per-token can vary depending on prompt/control tokens and model family.
- We can match or beat list-per-token for comparable model variants at scale once volume is committed; we often compete on predictable economics (true-ups, rollovers) rather than raw list price alone.

Next steps: I've attached a draft commercial summary (pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx) and a short exec blurb. If you like Option B as a working path, we can run this through legal next week and provide a redline-ready MSA + a DocuSign envelope template.

Quick Q for procurement: do you need a PO before signing or after DocuSign? That changes how we structure the prepayment flow.

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-20T20:15:00Z
Subject: FW: BluePeak pricing ask — recommended commercial stance

Ben / Karthik —

Saw Alex's thread and Ben's initial structure. From finance/legal perspective my recommendations below; I've run a quick margin and risk sensitivity against our cost model (attached brief).

Recommendation (leaning conservative but competitive):
1) Offer Option B (6-month credit-swap) as the procurement-friendly path to win now, with an explicit conversion window to Option A (12-month reserved slab) at a pre-agreed uplift. Terms: 10% discount on standard hosted list for the credit-swap prepay; minimum prepay = 4 months of expected usage based on current run-rate (we set a floor at $XXXk).
2) Include a 90-day performance checkpoint. If we miss latency SLOs by > 15% for two consecutive weeks, we commit to a service credit + assistance migrating any live traffic to a fallback model/region. Avoid open-ended rollback clauses — instead, offer a timeboxed remediation & migration credit (keeps legal simple).
3) Do not offer unconditional parity with public cloud endpoints (OpenAI/Anthropic). Instead: offer a price-match clause to verified written offers that meets the same scope (same model family, same SLA, and same data residency). We require their quote and term sheet as evidence.
4) Approve reduced payment terms: Net 30 for credit-swap customers on the first year only if Finance signs off on the PO timing.

Margin impact: a 10% discount on hosted list with the prepay floor leaves ~22% gross margin on projected Q3 run-rates — acceptable for landing this account given expansion potential.

Karthik — can you confirm sales ok with the prepay floor and the verification clause? Ben — if you get Alex to sign to Option B verbally, I'll prepare the legal brief and the finance approval note for a Net 30/PO flow.

Thanks —
Lauren
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference

Attachment: finance_brief_bluepeak_Q3.pdf


From: Alex Chen <alex.chen@bluepeak.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@bluepeak.com>
Date: 2026-07-21T09:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Exploring a commit credit swap vs per-token parity with OpenAI

Ben / Team —

Thanks — appreciate the fast turnaround and the two-option frame. Procurement reviewed internally and here's what they can sign up for today:

- We will accept Option B (6-month credit-swap) with the 10% hosted list discount, subject to:
  * Inclusion of the 90-day performance checkpoint and a written SLA remediation path (we prefer a specific numeric latency SLO in the docs)
  * A documented price-match clause that triggers only with a verified written offer (same scope), as Lauren proposed
  * A short data residency addendum (US-only region for the first year) and a DocuSign-ready MSA addendum for the performance metrics

We can issue a PO once we receive the DocuSign and the final payment instructions. Also: we'd like a guaranteed migration assistance clause (30 hours of engineering help) if performance forces us to switch during the 90-day checkout.

We compared the math to Anthropic "Claude Instant" and OpenAI pricing; your Option B effective rates look favorable enough for us to move forward. Please send the redline-ready MSA + DocuSign link and the one-page commercial term summary.

Alex
Director, Platform Engineering
BluePeak Analytics

Attachment: exec_summary_bluepeak.pdf

On Tue, Jul 20, 2026 at 18:00 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Alex —
> 
> Thanks for the clear ask and the RFP. We can do a concise set of options you can take to procurement/legal. High level (see attached pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx for the math):
> 
> Option A — 12-month committed slab
> - 500M token committed over 12 months (monthly true-up), effective per-token: $0.00030 (applies to text generation workloads)
> - SLA: 99.9% regional availability, priority routing, can include on-call escalation
> - Rollover: up to 10% unused credits can roll quarter-to-quarter for the first year
> 
> Option B — 6-month credit-swap (prepaid usage credits)
> - Prepay $X for 6 months worth of credits; effective per-token: $0.00035 (10% discount vs our standard hosted list)
> - Credits consumed first; no overage discount beyond credits; unused credits roll for 60 days after term end
> - Includes a performance review at 90 days with an SLA remediation clause (we can add a rollback / migration assistance clause if latency or throughput fall below agreed thresholds)
> 
> Ben Carter
> Enterprise Sales
> Redwood Inference

title: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Sana Patel <sana@deepmark.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Hi Sana —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion, DeepMark is aiming to spin up a dedicated APAC pool to improve inference latency for your enterprise customers in Singapore and Sydney. To make the pilot predictable on our side I outlined an initial ask and a couple of scheduling constraints in the attached doc (DeepMark_APAC_capacity_request.docx).

High level ask:
- Start with a 32-GPU reserved pool (4x8-GPU nodes) in APAC region for a 3-month pilot.
- Target sustained throughput: ~450 requests/minute with median p95 latency <= 220ms for completion endpoints.
- Expected token mix: 70% short completion (<=128 tokens), 30% longer chat sessions (avg 600 tokens).

Timeline:
- We understand you want pilot to start by 1 Aug; to hit that we need confirmation by 20 Jul and a quick PO so we can schedule capacity provisioning with our partners.

Could you confirm the numbers above and if there's any preferred availability window (SLA tier) we should provision? I copied Lauren (regional ops) to weigh in on placement and Marcus for the estimator.

Thanks —
Camila
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: DeepMark_APAC_capacity_request.docx (docx, 54kb)


From: Sana Patel <sana@deepmark.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: DeepMark Infra <infra@deepmark.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 02:04:00 +0800
Subject: Re: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Hi Camila —

Thanks for the clear summary. The numbers look reasonable as a starting point. A couple of clarifications from our side:

1) The 32-GPU pool is our minimum acceptable for the first 3 weeks of ramp; after that we expect traffic to grow by ~40% if the pilot performs as hoped. Can you model +40% headroom and a burst policy?
2) We need the option to enable HSM-backed KMS for keys (we'll send the KMS admin contact once procurement loops in).
3) Is there a way to use smaller GPU instances (A100 40GB vs. 80GB) in the initial pool to reduce cost while meeting latency SLOs?

We'll circulate a short internal approval today and route PO next week. Attaching our internal capacity sketch (see capacity_plan_apac_v1.xlsx).

Sana
Sana Patel
VP Infrastructure, DeepMark
sana@deepmark.ai

Attachment: capacity_plan_apac_v1.xlsx (xlsx, 22kb)


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Folks —

Forwarding DeepMark's ask and their capacity sketch. Key asks: 32 GPUs start, +40% headroom modeling, ability to prefer 40GB A100 vs 80GB to reduce unit cost, and HSM KMS. Pilot target start: ~1 Aug pending PO.

Marcus: can you run an initial cost/latency estimate for two configurations (8xA100-80GB nodes vs. 16xA100-40GB nodes), assuming the token mix Sana provided? Please include expected p95 latency and projected monthly token cost.

Vivek: can you confirm APAC provisioning lead time for a reserved pool and call out any capacity constraints in Singapore vs. Sydney? If APAC is tight, suggest fallback (e.g., Tokyo) and any cross-region egress impacts.

Lauren: please advise on org-level placement (we've done similar pilots but want to ensure we don't oversubscribe the small APAC pool next month).

Thanks —
Camila

-----Original Message-----
From: Sana Patel <sana@deepmark.ai>
Sent: Thu, Jul 16, 2026 at 02:04 AM +0800
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: DeepMark Infra <infra@deepmark.ai>
Subject: Re: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Camila / team —

Quick estimator based on token mix and our throughput model:
- Option A (4x8-GPU nodes, A100-80GB): baseline 32 GPUs. Estimated sustained throughput: ~520 req/minute. Projected p95 latency: ~180-230ms depending on concurrency and KV cache hit rate. Monthly cost estimate: ~$38k (GPU runtime + networking + premium APAC region uplift).
- Option B (8x4-GPU nodes, A100-40GB): equivalent vGPU count but finer-grained nodes. Pros: better packing for short jobs, lower per-node idle cost; Cons: slightly higher cross-node KV replication overhead may add ~10-15ms p95. Estimated sustained throughput: ~480 req/minute. Projected p95 latency: ~200-250ms. Monthly cost estimate: ~$31k.

Modeling +40% headroom moves Option A to ~45 GPUs effective (add 1 extra 8-GPU node) and Option B to the equivalent of ~11x4-GPU nodes; costs scale roughly linearly (Option A + ~$9k, Option B + ~$7k).

Notes:
- HSM-backed KMS is supported but requires a private deployment/Private control-plane or specific enterprise entitlements; if DeepMark wants hosted with HSM we need an SOC2-compliant integration path and likely a short lead time for certs.
- APAC inventory: Singapore is constrained in our partner pool in late July; Sydney has capacity but higher egress costs. Tokyo is the safest for guaranteed start by 1 Aug with comparable latency to Sydney for most Singapore-based customers.

I'll attach a quick spreadsheet with the numbers (will add to the shared Drive link).

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

Attachment: (spreadsheet added to Drive)


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 08:45:00 +0530
Subject: Re: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Team —

Confirming capacity constraints and timeline:
- Singapore partner pool: short-term constraint due to two other reserved launches the week of 25 Jul. Lead time for new reserved pool in SIN: ~10 business days from PO + capacity confirmation. Not safe for 1 Aug start.
- Sydney: available, lead time ~6 business days; egress uplift ~8-12% on billable cost.
- Tokyo: immediately available, lead time ~3 business days and lowest lead-time risk. Latency to SG is ~25-40ms higher than local SIN but still within DeepMark's 220ms p95 if we size per Marcus's Option B.

Recommendation: propose Tokyo start (we can migrate to SIN once capacity frees up in late Aug). Offer the following rollout plan to DeepMark:
- Phase 1 (pilot): Tokyo-based 32-GPU pool as 8x4-GPU nodes (A100-40GB) to start by 24 Jul (PO needed by 21 Jul).
- Phase 2 (scale): add +40% capacity week 4 and evaluate migration to Singapore if SIN inventory frees.

Lauren — happy to coordinate the temporary Sydney/Tokyo cost delta and to draft a short migration MOP.

Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
Director, Capacity Planning, Redwood Inference
vivek@redwood.ai


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Sana Patel <sana@deepmark.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: APAC capacity ask - DeepMark initial sizing & timeline

Sana —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Based on internal estimates and capacity checks, our recommended approach to hit your 1 Aug soft-start is:

1) Start in Tokyo with Option B (8x4-GPU nodes using A100-40GB) to meet the cost/latency balance.
2) Include a roll-forward +40% capacity trigger at week 4 tied to utilization thresholds (we'll add the exact Trigger in the draft SOW).
3) We can support HSM KMS but that will require a short enterprise onboarding flow; we can run the pilot with customer-managed keys stored in your KMS and cutover to HSM in Phase 2.

Next steps:
- Confirm PO/PO intent by 21 Jul to guarantee 24 Jul provisioning.
- I'll share a one-page SOW and the migration plan (SOW_template_for_deepmark.pdf attached).

If that sounds good I'll prepare the SOW and bring procurement/legal into a short loop.

Best —
Camila

Attachment: SOW_template_for_deepmark.pdf (pdf, 46kb)

Hi Will —

Thanks again for the great demo last week. GreenTech.ai's model-forward pipeline with your telemetry use case looks like a perfect fit for Redwood's low-latency serving. Azure Marketplace has asked us for a one-line customer quote and logo permission so we can complete the co-sell badge application. title: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@vortexai.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Hi Priya,

Thanks again for the time last week. As you noted, Vortex's Q3 product ramp is driving higher service-level peak TPS and longer context lengths which will stress the current Dedicated fleet in July–September. We want to put together a concise decision matrix to help you evaluate three distinct approaches before the renewal conversation:

- Option 1 — Incremental add: add 8x A800s to the committed pool (predictable throughput).
- Option 2 — Burst / on-demand pool: keep current commit and rely on burst capacity during peaks.
- Option 3 — Rightsize + credits: reduce steady commit but add guaranteed burst credits (cost trade).

Can you attach the latest telemetry (p99 token latency, tail concurrency, daily peak GPU-hours) and the business targets you expect for Sept–Nov (TPS growth, latency SLO hard limits)? I've attached the Q2 usage CSV we extracted from our console for reference.

If you prefer, we can run a short modeling session this week with our infra team to fill the matrix with latency/cost/sla rows. What time works for you?

Best,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0126

Attachment: usage_report_q2.csv (CSV; 42KB)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@vortexai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Hi Ben,

Sorry for the terse reply — heads down preparing the Q3 roadmap. I've attached a short telemetry summary (p50/p95/p99 latencies, daily GPU-hours, batch sizes) and a one-page product ramp slide. High-level context: we expect a staged feature launch starting 1 Sep, with an expected 45% traffic increase to the inference tier by mid-October. Latency SLO remains p95 < 220ms, p99 < 600ms.

We're open to a hybrid approach if it keeps unit costs sensible. Please include an estimated cost delta for each option (both delta monthly and per-token unit).

Thanks,

Priya Natarajan
VP, Platform & Ops | Vortex Analytics
priya@vortexai.com

Attachment: vortex_telemetry_summary.pdf (PDF; 180KB)

On Tue, 14 Jul 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Priya,
> 
> Thanks again for the time last week. As you noted, Vortex's Q3 product ramp is driving higher service-level peak TPS and longer context lengths which will stress the current Dedicated fleet in July–September....


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 08:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Kimberly / Markus — looping you both in. Vortex (Priya) sent Q3 ramp guidance and telemetry; they want a short decision matrix showing cost, latency impact, and operational effort for three approaches (incremental add, burst pool, rightsize+credits).

Ask: can you draft a 1–2 page matrix we can send to Priya by Friday? Key rows: incremental cost (monthly), expected p99 impact at peak, rollout lead time, SLA exposure, and ops complexity. Please annotate assumptions (context lengths, batch sizes from the telemetry PDF).

I added a lightweight options template (attached) — please populate and add recommended next steps. If a short modeling session is helpful, propose 2x 30-minute slots Thu/Fri.

Thanks — Ben

Attachment: options_template.xlsx (XLSX; 28KB)

From: Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 10:47:00 +02:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Ben / Kimberly — I put together a quick technical delta for the three approaches based on Vortex's telemetry. Highlights:

- Option 1 (add 8x A800):
  * Steady throughput increases ~38% at median batch sizes; tail p99 improves ~12–18% during peak windows.
  * Lead time: ~3 business days to provision into their Dedicated pool once PO is confirmed (we can reserve a tranche).
  * Operational: minimal; standard rollout and health checks.

- Option 2 (burst pool):
  * Provides headroom for infrequent ~2–3x peak spikes; cost efficient if peaks are brief.
  * p99 during long sustained peaks degrades (cache warm start costs), so not ideal if the ramp is sustained for weeks.
  * Lead time: immediate (burst capacity active), but requires runway for burst credits if spikes persist.

- Option 3 (rightsize + credits):
  * Lowers steady spend but relies on guaranteed credits; good if traffic profile is highly spiky and predictable.
  * Higher coordination overhead (billing/contract amendment).

I've added a sample performance simulation using the p99 curves from their PDF; see the summary below and the attached matrix in the next internal email. Also linking the capacity model I used: https://redwood.share/space/vortex-capacity-model

Technical ask for modeling session: need one 30m slot with Vortex infra + one 30m internal to validate assumptions.

— Markus
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
markus_klein@redwood.com

On Wed, 15 Jul 2026 at 08:20, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Kimberly / Markus — looping you both in. Vortex (Priya) sent Q3 ramp guidance and telemetry...


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Team — attached is the consolidated decision matrix (v1). Executive summary:

- Recommendation: Phased approach — provision 4x A800s now (July) + 4x A800s as a committed tranche in Sept if telemetry matches forecast. Backstop with a 200-hour burst credit pool for September spikes.
- Rationale: Balances predictable latency improvement with smaller near-term commitment; reduces immediate cash outlay and preserves negotiation leverage at renewal.
- Cost delta (approx): +$42k/month for first tranche; +$40k/month for the Sept tranche (est). Burst credits ~ $6k/month if used intermittently.
- Ops notes: 3-day provisioning lead time per tranche; monitoring alert for tail latency + autoscaling thresholds required.

Next steps proposed for Priya: sign off on phased plan by Mon 20 Jul; if signed, we prepare PO and schedule provisioning windows. Also propose a 30m modeling session with Markus to validate p99 under simulated ramp.

Attachment: vortex_decision_matrix_v1.xlsx (XLSX; 95KB)

Kimberly Park
Senior Sales Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com

On Thu, 16 Jul 2026 at 10:47, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com> wrote:
> Ben / Kimberly — I put together a quick technical delta for the three approaches based on Vortex's telemetry...

Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 11:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Capacity decision matrix: GPU addition options for upcoming commit

Ben / Kimberly — thanks for the rapid turn. The phased approach makes sense given our projected ramp. We approve the pilot: provision the first tranche (4x A800) in late July and keep the burst credit backstop for Sep.

Please send the PO template and a short onboarding checklist for the provisioning window. Also schedule the 30m modeling session with Markus — Thu 23 Jul 10:00am ET works on our side.

title: Request: Exec sign-off for 25% Hosted API concession — SuperCharge.ai
preview:
From: Alejandra Ruiz <alejandra.ruiz@supercharge.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19 09:12 -0700
Subject: Hosted API pricing ask — SuperCharge pilot

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the team was impressed with the latency numbers and the per-token cost estimates. We walked the execs through a 6-month pilot and they asked whether we can get a deeper introductory rate to help justify the initial integration work.

Current ask: 25% off list Hosted API usage for the first 6 months (applies to generation tokens only), with a committed minimum of $120k billed during the pilot period.

Key context:
- We expect a 6x monthly usage ramp over the pilot and plan to go into a paid expansion with dedicated capacity if initial performance aligns with SLAs.
- We will sign a SOW and provide usage forecasts (attached).
- Security/compliance: we need the ability to enable VPC peering for the expansion (long-term ask).

Can you pass this to the right person for exec clearance? We understand this is a material concession but this is strategic for SuperCharge — they bring a channel partnership and co-marketing commitment.

Appreciate any visibility and rough timing.

Best,
Alejandra Ruiz
Head of Partnerships | SuperCharge.ai
---
Attachments: Usage_Forecast_Q3-Q4_2026.xlsx, SuperCharge_SOW_v1.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19 11:04 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Hosted API pricing ask — SuperCharge pilot

Team — forwarding inbound from SuperCharge (Alejandra). Short background: this is a strategic channel partner that committed to a co-sell pilot; they will immediately surface three enterprise prospects if the pilot proves productive.

Ask: approval for a 25% one-time concession off Hosted API usage (generation token pricing) for a 6-month pilot. We will require a $120k minimum guarantee and a clause that automatically transitions to list pricing after month 6 unless both parties agree to extend.

Revenue impact estimate (Rishi): based on their forecast the concession reduces gross Rev by ~$30k over 6 months but creates an expansion opportunity worth an estimated $600k ARR if both pilot and co-sell come through.

Legal (Marissa) — attached is a draft SOW and our suggested redlines; main risk items: inbound data retention, indemnities, and explicit fallback to list pricing.

Avery — can you confirm customer timeline (they want a signed agreement in 10 business days).

Requesting exec sign-off: Mina, do you approve this concession under the following constraints?
1) $120k minimum pilot guarantee collected up front or net-30 with finance holdback.
2) Auto-revert to list pricing after month 6 unless extended by documented mutual agreement.
3) No additional infra commitments until expansion (VPC peering only after Dedicated purchase).

Happy to jump on a quick call to walk through numbers.

Thanks,
Ben
Director, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19 13:28 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API pricing ask — SuperCharge pilot

Ben — thanks for the summary. I reviewed the forecast and legal redlines. High-level decision: APPROVE the 25% hosted usage concession for the 6-month pilot subject to the constraints you listed. Additional conditions:

- Finance must collect the $120k minimum up front as a deposit (we can credit against invoices). No net-30 exceptions unless Finance and I sign off separately.
- Include a hard clause that pricing auto-reverts at day 181 to list rates and that any extended discount requires executive re-approval.
- Require a short performance SLA (p95 latency < 250ms) in the SOW for the pilot phase; if SLA materially misses twice, we reserve the right to terminate pilot without penalties.
- Legal to sign off redlines by EOD 7/22; Marissa, priority please.

Ben — go ahead and send the approved terms to the customer and get the deposit wired. Copy me on the DocuSign envelope.

Mina
VP Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Alejandra Ruiz <alejandra.ruiz@supercharge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19 14:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API pricing ask — SuperCharge pilot

Alejandra — thanks for the clear ask and the forecast — appreciate the partnership framing. Good news: our leadership approved a one-time 25% concession on Hosted API generation pricing for a 6-month pilot under the following binding terms (summary):

- Concession: 25% off list Hosted API generation token pricing for months 0–6.
- Commitment: $120,000 pilot minimum, to be provided as an upfront deposit credited to invoices.
- Auto-revert: pricing returns to list on day 181 automatically unless both parties execute a written extension approved by Redwood execs.

title: Preemptive fallback & throughput safeguard proposal — Zephyra
preview:
From: Charlotte Nguyen <charlotte@zephyra.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: contracts@zephyra.ai
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify throughput protections and fallback during peak — Zephyra/Redwood Dedicated

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our infra and procurement teams reviewed the Dedicated SKU and want to lock down the operational behavior for spike scenarios before final sign-off. Two items they specifically asked us to confirm:

1) If our reserved GPUs hit sustained saturation (e.g., 95%+ for >5m), what deterministic fallback will Redwood take? Will requests be queued, rejected, or routed to a lower-capacity instance automatically?

2) We need a clear crediting / remediation mechanism if observed throughput falls below the committed floor in the contract window.

We want an operational runbook + short addendum to the SOW that clarifies these behaviors — preferably something our contracts team can attach when we execute. Can you and your ops folks send a proposed runbook this week? Procurement would need it for compliance and legal.

Appreciate it,
Charlotte
Senior Platform Engineer
Zephyra AI

[P.S. procurement@zenithai.com is their procurement contact — looping them in for timing.]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Charlotte Nguyen <charlotte@zephyra.ai>, contracts@zephyra.ai
Date: Tue, 13 Jul 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Clarify throughput protections and fallback during peak — Zephyra/Redwood Dedicated

Team — forwarding Zephyra's request. They want a concise operational runbook + contract addendum describing exactly what we do when a tenant's reserved GPU pool hits saturation and how we credit for shortfalls. High priority for the current negotiation.

Quick asks:
- Can ops provide the preferred fallback hierarchy (route to compatible variant / queue / reject) and the gating thresholds?
- Diego/Infra: can you propose measurable SLO language + how we calculate credit (tokens lost / time window)?
- Laura/Legal: we'll need a one-page addendum that legal can attach to the SOW; procurement wants this by next week.

I've attached a first-pass runbook draft as a starting point (safeguard-runbook-draft.pdf). Please review inline and let me know redlines. I'll then send their procurement a polished addendum and a short explanation for the contracts team.

Thanks,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Att: safeguard-runbook-draft.pdf

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Charlotte Nguyen <charlotte@zephyra.ai>, contracts@zephyra.ai
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2026 14:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Clarify throughput protections and fallback during peak — Zephyra/Redwood Dedicated

Ben / all,

Thanks — walked through the draft with infra and SRE. High-level technical proposal below (suitable for a one-page runbook + SLO appendix). I'll summarize first, then call out the precise wording we can give legal.

Summary (operational fallback hierarchy):
1) Soft shaping + local KV prefix cache: interrupts are absorbed by per-tenant prefix cache and shaping for short bursts (<=120s). No customer-visible rejections during this window.
2) Model variant fallback: if sustained >120s and the tenant's requests exceed configured concurrency, route requests to a compatible quantized variant with equivalent semantic surface but lower throughput cost. This keeps latency predictable.
3) Grace queue & backpressure: if variant fallback isn't available (e.g., model not onboarded), requests enter a grace queue (FIFO, max 30s wait) with token-based admission. If the queue depth hits threshold, we begin shedding new low-priority requests with 429 + Retry-After.
4) Cross-tenant burst loans (optional): with a contractual opt-in, we can allow limited burst capacity from a shared pool for short intervals (billing at premium rate).

SLO / credit proposal (measurable):
- Committed throughput floor: X tokens/sec averaged over 10-minute windows (value defined in the SOW).
- Failure mode: any 10-minute window where delivered throughput < 90% of committed floor counts as a breach.
- Remediation: for each breached window, customer receives a credit equal to 5x the estimated token delta cost (capped monthly at 10% of monthly committed spend). Credits appear as invoice adjustments in the next billing cycle.

Queueing behavior wording (for the addendum):
"During periods of sustained resource saturation Redwood will attempt the following, in order: (i) local prefix caching and request shaping for short bursts; (ii) routing to compatible model variants; (iii) brief FIFO grace queuing with admission control; and (iv) controlled shedding of low-priority requests (429 responses) when queue limits are reached. Customers may opt into cross-tenant burst loans under separate terms."

Notes / caveats:
- We must define "compatible variant" as a model variant verified by Redwood to be functionally equivalent for the customer's primary workloads; ops will maintain a compatibility matrix.
- Time windows and thresholds above are adjustable and should be parameterized in the SOW per customer needs.

I'll attach an ops-friendly version of this text and propose precise numbers for Zephyra once Ben confirms desired commitment level (tokens/sec or requests/sec and the monthly committed spend).

- Diego
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference
