From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 5 Jul 2026 13:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: FinVest pricing + context profile — action items

Camila / Vanessa — quick pricing sketch and ops notes to bring to the deep-dive. Numbers are intentionally conservative for hosted API; reserved commits and Dedicated lower the blended rate.

Assumptions and breakdown
- Baseline split: 70% 8k, 20% 32k, 10% 128k (per call)
- Baseline volume: 570M combined tokens / month (150M prompts, 420M completions)

Hosted list-rate sketch (per 1M tokens, combined prompt+completion):
- 8k context: $120 / 1M
- 32k context: $160 / 1M
- 128k context: $300 / 1M

Blended baseline math (weights applied):
- Blended price = 0.70*$120 + 0.20*$160 + 0.10*$300 = $84 + $32 + $30 = $146 / 1M
- Baseline monthly cost = 570 * $146 = $83,220 / month

Targeting $90 / 1M (client target) — commit options to approach target
- 12-month reserved commit (example): 40% discount on hosted list → blended ~$87.6 / 1M (close to $90). Requires a 12-mo commit at ~70% of baseline (i.e., ~400M tokens/month guaranteed).
- Dedicated pool + committed throughput: fixed VM+infrastructure fee (example) ~$35k/month + run rate of $40 / 1M tokens for compute-heavy usage → blended depends on utilization; at baseline this gets near $70–$85 / 1M if utilization is high.

Peak month example (1.2B tokens):
- If on hosted list rates: 1200 * $146 = $175,200
- With 12-mo commit and tiered overage pricing, peak month could be closer to $95–$110 / 1M depending on tier sizes

Operational notes
- 128k requests are heavy on memory and KV cache — recommend a small canary (5–10% of 128k traffic) on Dedicated or a larger instance type to validate perf before full cutover.
- Autoscaling windows should be pre-warmed for expected earnings windows; we can add a scripted burst policy for 48hr peaks.

Deliverables for deep-dive (Tue 7/7 10am PT)
- Short slide: pricing math and commit scenarios (I’ll bring the Excel sheet).
- Ops runbook draft for 128k canary + KMS notes for Private deployment (Vanessa to add).
- Draft quote (Camila) and example contract language for minimum monthly commit.

Attachments: draft_pricing_sheet.xlsx (inline per-email). I’ll follow with a spreadsheet showing per-day cost curves.

Marcus

marcus@redwoodinference.com

title: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Dwyer <claire@kiteworks.com>, Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks

Thanks again for the time earlier, Claire and Samir — quick recap and a concrete pilot proposal so we can move quickly. I pasted the short version below and attached a one-page playbook (redwood_pilot_playbook_v1.pdf).

Call recap (key points)
- Goal: evaluate Redwood inference for secure document search and assisted redaction within Kiteworks workflows.
- Primary success criteria: 1) Relevant retrieval precision >= 78% on your test queries, 2) average response latency <= 300ms p50 for single-document queries in the pilot config, 3) no exfiltration incidents and audit logs available for all calls.
- Scope we discussed: 10k documents (mixed PDFs and text), SSO via SAML, network-restricted private deployment option, and ephemeral logging for pilot evaluation.

Proposed 3-week sprint-style pilot (timeline)
- Week 0 (prep, 3–5 days): legal/POC access, sample dataset handoff, sign NDA + procurement PO.
- Week 1 (baseline & ingest): ingest sample set, baseline search runs (owner: Kiteworks + Redwood CS).
- Week 2 (tuning & test harness): run 5 evaluation suites, tune prompts/quantization profile, measure latency/cost.
- Week 3 (final validation & handoff): run acceptance tests, produce report with next-step recommendations.

Roles & ownership
- Kiteworks: dataset delivery (Samir), procurement sign-off, internal eval stakeholders.
- Redwood: pilot setup (Monica), SRE contact for private connectivity (Karthik), weekly status.

Deliverables
- Ingested dataset + runbook, evaluation dashboard, acceptance report (pass/fail vs success criteria), cost estimate for production scale.

Immediate next steps
1) Please confirm procurement contact and a best-available kickoff window next week (I have availability Tue/Wed morning PT).
2) Samir — can you confirm we can get a 3–5k sample set exported by Monday?
3) Claire — any mandatory compliance evidence you need up front other than the security checklist?

Attachments: redwood_pilot_playbook_v1.pdf (one-page plan)

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0128



From: Claire Dwyer <claire@kiteworks.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 17:04:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks

Thanks Ben — this is really helpful and aligns with our thinking. A couple quick clarifications before we lock dates: 

1) Data handling / retention: For the pilot we need logs retained for 90 days and a clear configurable option to disable persistent storage of payloads. Is Redwood able to support ephemeral request retention for the pilot?

2) SSO & SCIM: We'll want SAML for the console and SCIM for user provisioning in the pilot so our security team can test RBAC flows. Is that included in the private deployment or would that add lead time?

3) Security evidence: attaching our security questionnaire (kiteworks_security_checklist.xlsx). If you can pre-fill the SOC-related items that would speed our internal review. 

Samir — can you confirm sample set availability? We should include a handful of realistic queries (50) for the precision tests.

On timing, Tue/Wed AM PT should work — Claire prefers Wed morning if that's still open.

Best,
Claire
Claire Dwyer
Head of Platform Security | Kiteworks
claire@kiteworks.com



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Dwyer <claire@kiteworks.com>, Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2026 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks

Claire — thanks, quick answers below and I'll have Monica fill the security questionnaire items next.

1) Retention: Yes — we can configure ephemeral request retention for the pilot (default 0 days) and also provide 90-day log export if you want. That configuration will be scoped in the playbook.
2) SAML + SCIM: Both supported. For private deployments SCIM requires a short config step (we'll need a SCIM endpoint and admin token from your side). That typically adds 2–4 business days if we have the endpoint ready prior to kickoff.
3) Security evidence: Monica will return the completed SOC and encryption sections in the checklist by EOD Monday.

Claire — Wed morning PT works; proposing Wed 07/07 9:30am PT for a 30-min kickoff (invite to follow).

Samir — can you share a 3–5k sample export and the 50 test queries by EOD Monday 07/05? If not, let us know expected timing.

I'll also attach a short network diagram and the private deployment checklist to the calendar invite.

- Ben



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Dwyer <claire@kiteworks.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Jul 2026 08:16:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks

Adding a short technical note from our infra side: 
- Ingest: preferred export format is newline-delimited JSON with document metadata (id, source, created_at). PDFs are fine; we'll extract text during ingest.
- Network: for private deployment we'll need an allowlist of 3 IPs (outbound) and a site-to-site VPN or AWS VPC peering details — happy to run through options on the kickoff call.
- Tokenization/cost: for the evaluation harness we recommend enabling KV cache for repeated queries to get realistic latency/cost numbers; caching is on by default but configurable.

If you have strict PII removal constraints we can add a pre-ingest redaction step — flag it and we'll include an SOW item.

Karthik
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai



From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Dwyer <claire@kiteworks.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>
Date: Mon, 06 Jul 2026 09:32:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Structured recap and sprint-style pilot proposal — Kiteworks

Hi all — quick closing items from CS so we can lock the kickoff: 
1) I'll own pilot orchestration and the evaluation dashboard delivery.
2) Claire — thanks for the checklist; I'll return the SOC/encryption answers by EOD today and share the pilot runbook link so your security team can review.
3) Ben — confirmed for Wed 07/07 9:30am PT. I'll invite our SRE and the private-deploy engineer to the call.

Samir — can you confirm sample export ETA? If you can't make Monday, Wed morning still works as long as we have at least 1k records to start ingestion during Week 1.

Looking forward — Monica
Monica Patel
Customer Success | Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.ai

title: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Hi Kim / Marissa —

Quick summary: NimbusHealth is seeing a sustained p95 increase for inference on model_phi v3.14 that started after the 2026-07-03 canary push. Their production p95 climbed from ~220ms to ~560ms for a subset of long-sequence traffic (~5–6% of requests). They are requesting a temporary SLA tolerance while we investigate.

I attached the p95 export and perf graphs (taken across their prod region us-west-2). Key ask: they want a provisional 72-hour tolerance on p95 (to avoid automatic downstream throttles) and a traffic pin to keep them on the older variant if we push a fast rollback.

What I need from you:
- Kim: authorization to approve a provisional SLA tolerance window for NimbusHealth (72 hours) OR a shorter 48-hour conditional window if we can commit to a rollback plan within that time.
- Marissa: advice on customer credit implications if we approve the tolerance, and whether legal should be looped for a carveout addendum.
- Marcus: quick ops checklist for a safe rollback and rollback-if-unresolved trigger.

Attachments: 2026-07-04_nimbus_p95_report.csv, model_phi_v3.14_perf_graphs.pdf

Proposed immediate mitigation steps (I can coordinate with Nimbus SRE):
- Pin 20% of their traffic to model_phi v3.13 (previous variant) as a fallback path (traffic fencing).
- Activate prefix KV caching for their hot prefixes (estimated ~10–15% improvement on those requests).
- Stand up an elevated monitoring dashboard (15-minute cadence) and mobile alerting to on-call.

I'll be on call this morning — can hop on quick with SRE and Nimbus if helpful.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

--
Attachment(s): 2026-07-04_nimbus_p95_report.csv (csv), model_phi_v3.14_perf_graphs.pdf (pdf)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Thanks for the heads-up, Ben. I reviewed the attached CSV and the PDF plots — the delta is non-trivial and concentrated on long-sequence requests, which aligns with our kernel/attenuation hypothesis for v3.14.

A few quick clarifying Qs before I can approve anything:
- Is the increase isolated to their region (us-west-2) or global? (the graphs look regional but I want confirmation)
- What's the customer impact in terms of revenue/critical workflows? Are they in a hard real-time path?
- Do we have an immediate rollback candidate that preserves semantic parity (v3.13)?

On tolerances: I'm open to a provisional 48-hour tolerance provided we get a written acceptance from Nimbus that they understand potential SLA credit consequences. If we can't produce a rollback plan that includes a tested canary within 24 hours, I won't sign a 72-hour blanket tolerance.

Marissa — can you size potential credits for a 48 vs 72-hour tolerance? If legal needs to draft a short-term carveout, tell me and I'll approve the emergency sign-off workflow.

Kim
Kimberly Park
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference

> Quick summary: NimbusHealth is seeing a sustained p95 increase for inference on model_phi v3.14...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

Kim / Ben —

Thanks for the detail. I agree with Kim's caution. From a finance/contracting perspective:
- A 48-hour tolerance with a documented rollback trigger is preferable. For a high-dollar account like Nimbus (NB-4832), a 72-hour blanket tolerance will likely require a short amendment and a recorded customer signoff.
- Estimated credit exposure if we grant a 48h tolerance: ~$3.2K (prorated per SLA terms). For 72h, ~$6.8K + potential churn risk if they lose confidence.

Conditions for my provisional approval (48 hours):
1) Marcus to publish a rollback playbook (30 mins) that includes a tested canary and an automated traffic pin.
2) Ben to secure a written acceptance from Katelyn (Nimbus SRE) confirming they agree to the provisional tolerance and understand credit implications.
3) Engineering (on-call infra) to commit to a 12-hour hotfix window or an explicit rollback if root cause not found.

Marcus — can you drop the playbook draft into the Drive link and confirm that the rollback is scripted (not manual) so we can avoid human delay?

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Success & Renewals | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Cc: Katelyn Price <katelyn.price@nimbushealth.com>, ops@nimbushealth.com
Date: Sat, 4 Jul 2026 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Temporary p95 tolerance & rollback gate — NimbusHealth (model_phi v3.14)

All —

I pulled a quick ops runbook and dropped a draft rollback_playbook_draft.md into the shared Drive folder (link in related links). Summary of the actionable items I can complete within the next 6 hours:
- Scripted rollback to v3.13 with traffic fencing baked in (automated route changes + canary at 5% then 20%).
- Smoke tests against Nimbus's long-sequence workloads (10 test sequences) — results expected within 45 minutes.
- Rapid rollback trigger: if p95 for the canary >350ms for two consecutive 10-minute windows we auto-execute the rollback.

I recommend approving the provisional 48-hour tolerance with the three conditions Marissa listed. I will also tag infra on-call and assign an incident owner.

Ben — please ask Katelyn to reply here with explicit acceptance (one-liner is fine) so I can flip the automation to the canary mode. Kim / Marissa — if you each approve that path, I'll flip the switches and commence validation.

Hi Aisha,

We're kicking off security intake for the Biomedix production integration and need a few items to close procurement and the security review by next Friday (2026-07-09):

- Full subprocessor inventory (vendor name, service provided, country, data access type)
- Copy of SOC 2 Type II (most recent) or SOC bridge letter if SOC is older than 12 months

title: ClearPath discovery + integration plan — SDK preference & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Emily Nguyen <emily.nguyen@clearpathlogistics.com>, Michael O'Neil <michael.oneil@clearpathlogistics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Recap: Discovery & integration fit — ClearPath / Redwood

Hi Emily / Michael — thanks again for your time earlier. Quick recap from our call and proposed next steps so we have alignment before the PoC kickoff.

High-level pain points you confirmed
- Search relevance for routing: embeddings drift when new SKUs are added; manual tuning is painful.
- Latency spikes during nightly batch reconciliations (some requests >500ms currently).
- Unit economics: long-term cost of embeddings + generation needs to be predictable for your volumes.

Current stack notes (as mentioned on the call)
- Backend: Python microservices (FastAPI) + Node frontend
- Search: Elasticsearch 8.2 for retrieval, custom ranking layer
- Infra: hybrid cloud; most training/on-prem GPUs for sensitive workloads; preferring private networking for PII datasets

Success criteria for a successful PoC (suggested)
1) 95th-pct latency for typical retrieval+generate flows <= 200ms
2) Matching-quality: embeddings-based recall lift >= 12% vs current baseline on the sample dataset
3) Clear estimates for token cost at scale (1M tokens/month cost target) and a plan to keep unit cost under that threshold

Integration & onboarding plan (proposed)
- SDKs: we support Python and Node officially. Recommend starting with Python (aligns with your backend) and adding Node for the UI team if desired.
- API compatibility: standard REST + streaming responses; supports prefix KV caching and automatic batching.
- Auth & networking: API keys by default; Private/VPC option supports mTLS and customer KMS for on-prem deployments.
- PoC timeline (4-week cadence)
  * Week 0: Kickoff / access & security checklist completed
  * Week 1: SDK install + ingest sample dataset; baseline runs
  * Week 2: Integrate retrieval/generate flow; iterate on prompts and embeddings
  * Week 3: Run evaluation tests and latency/load checks; capture cost estimates
  * Week 4: Final review, go/no-go & next steps (Dedicated capacity quote if moving to production)

Owners (Redwood):
- Ben Carter (AE) — PoC owner, mutual-action-plan driver
- Vivek Kulkarni (SE) — technical lead for integration and test harness
- Marissa Cole (CS) — onboarding + success criteria instrumentation

What we need from ClearPath to proceed
- Confirm SDK preference (Python or Node) and whether a private deployment is required for the PoC
- Completed security questionnaire (attached) or an NDA if you prefer that first
- Availability for a technical kickoff (30–45m) the week of July 6 — we proposed Tue/Wed as options

I've attached the security questionnaire you mentioned and a starter mutual action plan template. I'll put together a lightweight test dataset template for Week 1 and share early sample code.

Thanks — happy to adjust/remove anything if I mis-captured.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
https://www.redwood.inference.com

Attachments: ClearPath-Security-Questionnaire.pdf, mutual_action_plan_clearpath_v1.xlsx

From: Emily Nguyen <emily.nguyen@clearpathlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Michael O'Neil <michael.oneil@clearpathlogistics.com>
Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 09:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap: Discovery & integration fit — ClearPath / Redwood

Ben — thanks for the detailed recap, that looks right to us. A few clarifications:
- We'd like to start on Python (backend team can iterate faster there).
- For the PoC we prefer Redwood Private (VPC) because some of the SKU metadata cannot leave our VPC during evaluation. We understand there is a private onboarding checklist; please point us to it.
- Please also include an estimate for a short Dedicated-capacity spike for the end-of-month stress test — we'll need numbers to budget it.

I've attached the completed security questionnaire (answers in the form). Let me know if anything is missing.

Emily Nguyen
Head of Platform Engineering
ClearPath Logistics
emily.nguyen@clearpathlogistics.com
Attachment: ClearPath-Security-Questionnaire.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Emily Nguyen <emily.nguyen@clearpathlogistics.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Jul 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap: Discovery & integration fit — ClearPath / Redwood

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the completed questionnaire, Emily. A few follow-ups and concrete items from our side:

1) Private/VPC PoC: we can run the PoC in Redwood Private. For Week 0 we'll need network allowlists (CIDR + egress rules), certs for mTLS if you want hardened auth, and a temporary service account for ingestion. Vivek and Laura will send a short tech checklist by end of day.

2) Dedicated spike estimate: preliminary numbers for a 48-hour GPU spike (NVIDIA A10 equivalent) + throughput for 100k requests: USD 3,500 — this is a placeholder until we pin exact sequence lengths and batch settings. We will refine this after Week 1 baseline run.

3) SDK/package: attaching the Python SDK bundle (redwood_sdk_python_v0.9.1.zip) and a short example in the repo that shows streaming + prefix caching usage. For the PoC, recommended settings:
   - quantization: int8 profile to keep memory footprint low
   - batching: burst-friendly batch size 8 with dynamic flush at 50ms

4) Mutual action plan: draft is in Drive (link below). I've proposed milestone dates that align with the 4-week cadence; please review.

Proposed next meeting: Technical kickoff with Vivek + Laura and your engineering team — Tue, July 7 at 10:00am PT (30–45m). If that works, we'll send invites and the pre-kickoff checklist.

Thanks — we'll follow up with the network checklist and a short runbook for ingestion.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com
Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AaB-mutual-action-plan-clearpath

From: Michael O'Neil <michael.oneil@clearpathlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Emily Nguyen <emily.nguyen@clearpathlogistics.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap: Discovery & integration fit — ClearPath / Redwood

Ben — sounds good. Python first works for us. Confirming the Private/VPC route for the PoC. Emily and I are available on Tue, July 7 at 10am PT for the kickoff; please send calendar invite.

Also: can the SE session include a short walkthrough of the latency monitoring panels in Redwood Console? That will help our SREs understand the SLA implications.

Michael O'Neil
CTO | ClearPath Logistics

title: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Team —

Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. The deal is at signature (GF-3382) and procurement has signaled a hard gate: the pilot must demonstrate per-tenant QoS (latency SLO enforcement) and a short-term model residency/pinning guarantee for a 10-day activation window. They want this validated before committing the first monthly payment.

Key asks (from GF):
- Pin a model variant and provide a tenant-level QoS policy that limits end-to-end p95 latency to 250ms for a capped RPS.
- A short-term residency/pinning guarantee for the pilot (10 days) with rollback policy.
- A telemetry snapshot and acceptance test plan they can run during the pilot.

Why this matters: If we can offer a clear, timeboxed pin + QoS approach we keep GF in the deal (ACV $560k ARR annualized) and open the account to Dedicated expansion. They are sensitive to noisy neighbors and want deterministic performance for an internal trading workflow.

Proposed commercial trade: short-term SLA addendum for the 10-day pilot in exchange for commit to a Dedicated trial and a 6-month minimum if pilot meets acceptance criteria. I captured the mutual action plan and a compact requirements sheet (attached).

Asks for Product/Eng:
- Can we:
  1) Define a minimal implementation for tenant QoS enforcement that is safe to ship as a pilot (configurable policy + documented rollback).
  2) Approve an engineering timebox (estimate) and a tentative ETA for a pilot-ready config (non-invasive, feature-flagged path).

Timeline: GF expects pilot readiness week of 7/12. That gives ~2 weeks for an implementation plan + prioritization decision.

Requesting a quick sync to triage tradeoffs and document the minimal acceptance criteria. If this is a no-go, I need to tee up a fallback conversation with GF about a staged performance validation (paid POC).

Attaching: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf, galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

On Mon, 29 Jun 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. ...

Thanks for the clear summary, Ben. I reviewed the attached MAP and requirement sheet. A few clarifying points before I commit Eng budget/time: we need to be explicit about scope so we don't expand into a full multi-tenant QoS system for the pilot.

Suggested minimal scope (for pilot):
- Policy surface: route-level token that allows us to set a latency target and max concurrent requests per tenant (config only; enforcement via queueing + backpressure).
- Residency: a pinned worker pool assignment for the tenant for the 10-day window (best-effort on the first run; explicit fallback to shared pool if capacity drops).
- Observability: a telemetry snapshot + an automated acceptance test (RPS/latency runbook) that GF runs with our SRE.

Questions for Eng:
1) Marcus — rough estimate to implement a configurable route-level QoS token + basic backpressure enforcement (safe, feature-flagged path)?
2) Priyom — any existing infra that can support a short-term residency pin at the tenant layer without requiring a full tenancy isolation change?
3) Jordan — will the proposed approach require schema or SDK changes that client teams must pick up for the pilot?

If we can get rough estimates today, I can scope a short runbook and provide Ben an ETA to share with GF. If estimates look >2w, we should discuss the paid POC fallback.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Platform


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>, procurement@galaxyfinance.com
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Thanks, Laura — really helpful framing. Short answers inline to help prioritization: 

- Business impact: GF moves to signature upon receiving a deterministic pilot plan. If we miss the 7/12 readiness window they will push the deal and consider alternative vendors. This is a one-time strategic account with clear expansion potential.
- Commercial: GF has agreed to the short-term SLA addendum language; procurement is ready to sign once we confirm the technical approach and ETA.

Marcus / Priyom / Jordan — we need very rough T-shirt sizing today if possible (S / M / L + estimated engineering days). Laura has suggested a conservative minimal scope: route-level latency token + best-effort pinned worker pool + acceptance telemetry. Please reply with sizing and any blockers. If any of these are L (>3w), flag it and we will propose the paid POC alternative to GF (they indicated willingness).

Next steps I will take after your replies:
- Draft a short internal prioritization note for Eng leadership with expected impact and tradeoffs.
- If approved, coordinate a canary/feature-flag rollout and schedule the GF acceptance window (7/12 preferred).

Oliver / GF team — appreciate patience; pushing internally now and will confirm by EOD tomorrow.

Ben
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

--

> 
> Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. ...

Thanks for the clear summary, Ben. I reviewed the attached MAP and requirement sheet. A few clarifying points before I commit Eng budget/time: we need to be explicit about scope so we don't expand into a full multi-tenant QoS system for the pilot.

Suggested minimal scope (for pilot):
- Policy surface: route-level token that allows us to set a latency target and max concurrent requests per tenant (config only; enforcement via queueing + backpressure).
- Residency: a pinned worker pool assignment for the tenant for the 10-day window (best-effort on the first run; explicit fallback to shared pool if capacity drops).
- Observability: a telemetry snapshot + an automated acceptance test (RPS/latency runbook) that GF runs with our SRE.

Questions for Eng:
1) Marcus — rough estimate to implement a configurable route-level QoS token + basic backpressure enforcement (safe, feature-flagged path)?
2) Priyom — any existing infra that can support a short-term residency pin at the tenant layer without requiring a full tenancy isolation change?
3) Jordan — will the proposed approach require schema or SDK changes that client teams must pick up for the pilot?

If we can get rough estimates today, I can scope a short runbook and provide Ben an ETA to share with GF. If estimates look >2w, we should discuss the paid POC fallback.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Platform

Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Thanks, Laura — really helpful framing. Short answers inline to help prioritization: 

- Business impact: GF moves to signature upon receiving a deterministic pilot plan. If we miss the 7/12 readiness window they will push the deal and consider alternative vendors. This is a one-time strategic account with clear expansion potential.
- Commercial: GF has agreed to the short-term SLA addendum language; procurement is ready to sign once we confirm the technical approach and ETA.

Marcus / Priyom / Jordan — we need very rough T-shirt sizing today if possible (S / M / L + estimated engineering days). Laura has suggested a conservative minimal scope: route-level latency token + best-effort pinned worker pool + acceptance telemetry. Please reply with sizing and any blockers. If any of these are L (>3w), flag it and we will propose the paid POC alternative to GF (they indicated willingness).

Next steps I will take after your replies:
- Draft a short internal prioritization note for Eng leadership with expected impact and tradeoffs.
- If approved, coordinate a canary/feature-flag rollout and schedule the GF acceptance window (7/12 preferred).

Oliver / GF team — appreciate patience; pushing internally now and will confirm by EOD tomorrow.

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: MAP kickoff — Acme Cloud evaluation & roles

Team —

We go live on the Acme Cloud MAP kickoff with the customer on Wednesday 7/1 (10:00 PT). Before that, I want to lock down internal roles and the evaluation plan so we don’t get surprised mid-eval. Quick goals for this thread: 1) confirm RACI for MAP tasks, 2) tidy the evaluation checklist, 3) decide who will own procurement touchpoints during the legal review.

Attached: MAP-draft-v1.xlsx (high-level milestones), EvalChecklist-AcmeCloud.md (scoring rubric), RACI-roles-v2.pdf (proposal).

Proposed RACI highlights:
- Sales (I own executive sponsor & pricing cadence) — Account Exec
- Solution architecture & PoC validation — Monica (technical lead)
- Deployment/runbook & infra dependencies — Karthik (infra owner)
- Executive approvals & roadmap alignment — Marissa (customer success lead)
- Procurement & contract coordination — Laura (legal procurement partner)

Requests: please review the attachments and reply with any role objections or suggested changes by EOD Monday (6/29). If you see gaps in the evaluation checklist (e.g., data retention, audit logs, perf SLOs) call them out inline.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0189



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: MAP kickoff — Acme Cloud evaluation & roles

Ben — thanks. I reviewed MAP-draft-v1.xlsx and EvalChecklist. Two quick items from the technical side:

1) PoC scope: we should limit the initial PoC to a single API surface (chat generation) and one mid-size dataset to keep token costs predictable. Recommend calling out 3 scenarios (baseline, peak load, failure/retry) in the EvalChecklist.

2) Perf metrics: add 95th and 99th latency targets per region to the checklist — Acme will care about regional consistency. I can draft language and drop an updated EvalChecklist-AcmeCloud.md by EOD.

RACI: OK with me as technical lead. I’d like Karthik to be ‘R’ for infra readiness (networking, VPC peering), and I’ll be ‘A’ on feature acceptance.

Monica

title: Settlement summary and conditional credit for May overage
preview:
From: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Hi Ava,

Quick note — our billing team reconciled May usage and found a 42k token delta compared to the committed capacity lines. It's driven by a handful of high-concurrency jobs during our product launch week (May 18–22).

Can you share a final invoice or settlement figure we can route to procurement? We need it broken down: one-time overage charge, any credits you can apply, and whether there's a short-term capacity top-up option.

Attaching our usage export. Happy to hop on a short call if helpful.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Patel
Head of Ops, Finora Health
phone: (415) 555-0133

Attachments: Finora_usage_export_May2026.csv (CSV, 120KB)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Samira — thanks for the export and the heads-up. Diego and Grace copied for finance/CS context.

We ran the reconciler against our ingestion and confirm the ~42k token overage for May. Below is our preliminary settlement approach:

- One-time invoice: $3,150 (42k tokens at $0.075/token unit equivalent per your plan)
- Conditional credit: 50% of the one-time invoice ($1,575) if Finora signs the proposed 12-month capacity top-up amendment within 14 days (amendment adds 25% committed capacity and a 12-month term extension at current pricing).
- If amendment is not signed within 14 days, full one-time invoice will be issued and due in 30 days.

We can provide a formal PDF proposal and DocuSign link. Grace — can finance confirm the line-item and draft the PDF? Jonas — please prepare a short note on operational safeguards we can include (burst limits, alerting) so Samira's team can approve faster.

Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Attachments: none


From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Hi Samira, Ava —

I've attached a short settlement proposal PDF with the invoice line and the conditional credit language. The one-time invoice amount is $3,150; conditional credit of $1,575 applied as a credit memo to the next invoice once the amendment is fully executed and backdated to the start of the next billing cycle.

Payment terms: Net 30 on the invoice. Credit memo will appear within 2 billing cycles after signing and verification.

If you prefer the credit to be a direct refund instead of a credit memo, we can discuss that with finance but prefer credit memo to keep accounts tidy.

Diego
Diego Fernandez
Director, Finance Operations, Redwood Inference

Attachments: Redwood_settlement_proposal_06026.pdf (PDF, 48KB)


From: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 07:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Thanks, Diego — that looks reasonable. A couple quick clarifications before I take this to procurement:

1) Does the 12-month amendment change price per token for the new committed capacity? (We want rate certainty.)
2) If we sign, will the credit be visible on the invoice line-level so our procurement folks can reconcile easily?

If those are yes, we'll sign the amendment. Can you send the DocuSign link?

—Samira

On Wed, Jun 24, 2026 at 07:22 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
>
> [Quoted earlier message]
>


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Samira — quick answers from product/ops side:

1) Rate for the added committed capacity will remain at your current per-token unit price for the 12-month term. We'll include an explicit line in the amendment: \"committed capacity price fixed at current schedule for the duration of the amendment.\"
2) Credit visibility: the credit memo will include a line referencing the original invoice number and a short description (\"May 2026 Settlement Credit - RDW-4521\"). We will also attach the credit memo PDF to the next invoice in the billing portal.

Additionally, I've added two operational items to the amendment: 24/7 alerting when utilization exceeds 85% of committed capacity for three consecutive hours, and an optional burst bucket (prepaid) offer. That should reduce surprise spikes.

Jonas Weber
Sr. Product Ops, Redwood Inference



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Final — settlement confirmed and DocuSign link

Samira — thanks for the quick turnaround. Per our calls and the thread above, we have: 

- One-time invoice for May overage: $3,150 (issued today).
- Conditional credit: $1,575 (50% of invoice) to be issued as a credit memo against your next invoice once the amendment is executed and DocuSigned within 14 days.
- Amendment: 12-month capacity top-up (25% additional committed capacity), price fixed at current schedule. Operational safeguards included (alerts + optional burst bucket).

DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abcd-1234

Please route to procurement/billing. Once signed, Diego will backdate and post the credit memo. Grace will follow up with invoice numbers and a short reconciliation note for your AP team.

Thanks — looking forward to continuing to scale with Finora.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Attachments: Signed_Amendment_RDW-4521.pdf (PDF, 72KB)
